content: Let me read the compactor instructions and the task_pending.md file to understand what I need to do.

1	# task_pending.md Compactor
2	
3	You are a *pre-agent* that runs ahead of the plan agent. Your job is to shrink `.archon/task_pending.md` while preserving every dead end and every ongoing technical detail the plan agent will need.
4	
5	You are NOT the plan agent. You DO NOT change which targets are pending. You DO NOT add new attempts or annotations. You only rewrite existing content in a tighter form.
6	
7	## What you may write to
8	
9	- `.archon/task_pending.md` (read + write — this is your only target).
10	
11	## What you may NOT touch
12	
13	- Every other file in `.archon/`. Specifically: do not move entries to `task_done.md`, do not edit PROGRESS.md, do not touch USER_HINTS.md.
14	- Any `.lean` file, `blueprint/**`, `archon-protected.yaml`.
15	
16	## Workflow
17	
18	1. Read `.archon/task_pending.md` once.
19	2. Identify each pending target and its attempts/probes log.
20	3. Rewrite per the rules below.
21	4. One `Write` call to overwrite the file.
22	5. Reply with one line: `COMPACTED: <pre> -> <post> chars` or `UNCHANGED: <reason>`.
23	
24	## Information preservation — non-negotiable
25	
26	The plan agent uses task_pending.md to know:
27	- Which targets are still open
28	- What's been tried (so it doesn't re-assign the same dead-end approach)
29	- Which Mathlib gaps block which targets
30	
31	**Never drop or summarize away:**
32	- Documented dead ends (`tried X, failed because Y`)
33	- Mathlib infrastructure gaps surfaced during attempts
34	- User hints / authorisations that landed in this file
35	- Cross-references to specific Mathlib declarations the plan agent or refactor subagent should consider next time
36	- Any "next plan" suggestion an attempt left behind
37	
38	**When in doubt, KEEP.**
39	
40	## Per-entry compaction rules
41	
42	A pending entry typically looks like:
43	
44	```markdown
45	### `Foo.bar` (`Path/To/File.lean`)
46	- **Status**: open
47	- **Last attempt** (iter NNN): <multi-paragraph narrative>
48	- **Earlier attempts**:
49	  - iter MMM: <multi-paragraph narrative>
50	  - iter LLL: <multi-paragraph narrative>
51	- **Mathlib gaps**: ...
52	- **Notes**: ...
53	```
54	
55	Rewrite as:
56	
57	```markdown
58	### `Foo.bar` (`Path/To/File.lean`)
59	- **Status**: open
60	- **Last attempt** (iter NNN): <multi-paragraph narrative — VERBATIM>
61	- **Earlier attempts**:
62	  - iter MMM: <one-sentence outcome>
63	  - iter LLL: <one-sentence outcome>
64	- **Mathlib gaps**: <verbatim>
65	- **Notes**: <verbatim>
66	```
67	
68	In other words:
69	
70	- Keep the **last attempt verbatim** — that's what the plan agent will reason about.
71	- Compress every **earlier attempt** to one line. The one line must record **what was tried** and **why it failed** (the dead-end signal). Drop only restateable detail like exact tactic names, line numbers, intermediate goal states.
72	- Do not merge attempts across iterations into a single line — each iteration's attempt is a separate dead end the plan agent must not re-walk.
73	- "Mathlib gaps" / "Notes" / any user-hint-derived block: verbatim.
74	
75	If an entry has only one attempt, leave it untouched.
76	
77	If an entry has 5+ attempts, you may merge the *oldest two* into a range bullet (`- iter LLL-MMM: <one sentence covering both>`) only if they failed for the **same** reason. If reasons differ, keep separate.
78	
79	## What "one-sentence outcome" must include
80	
81	For each compressed attempt, the one-line summary MUST contain:
82	- iteration number
83	- approach name or short description
84	- failure mode (`Mathlib lacks <X>` / `proof obligation N reduces to false claim Y` / `prover stopped at sorry, no progress`)
85	
86	If you cannot express all three in one sentence, use two — never lose the failure mode.
87	
88	## Anti-patterns
89	
90	- Do not delete a "Mathlib gap" block because the plan agent might "have noticed it elsewhere." Trust nothing — keep it.
91	- Do not migrate any entry to `task_done.md`. That is the plan agent's call after verification.
92	- Do not add `## Status: closed` annotations. Leave status fields exactly as you found them.
93	- Do not invoke any subagent.
94	- Do not edit any other file.
95	
96	## Output rules
97	
98	- One `Write` call (full-file replace) after drafting the new content.
99	- Final assistant message:
100	  - `COMPACTED: <pre> -> <post> chars` on success
101	  - `UNCHANGED: <reason>` if nothing was worth compacting (and do NOT call Write)
102

1	# Index
2	<!-- One line per file. Update line numbers when the file changes. -->
3	
4	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` — **closed iter-011** (see `task_done.md`). 0 sorries. Status: DONE. The protected `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` was filled (term-mode one-liner `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`) under the user's `2026-05-07` `noncomputable` authorisation. The downstream `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (in `Jacobian.lean`) will eventually require `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` (Serre finiteness — Phase A step 6 *proper*); iter-028+ scaffolds the geometric-side input.
5	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — 5 sorries: `Jacobian` (L77), `instGrpObj` (L88), `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (L97), `instIsProper` (L104), `instGeometricallyIrreducible` (L114). Status: BLOCKED on Phase C, gated on `PicardFunctor.representable` (multi-iteration; FGA argument + `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` Mathlib gap).
6	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` — 3 sorries: `ofCurve` (L35), `comp_ofCurve` (L41), `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (L53). Status: BLOCKED on `Jacobian C` first; the *uniqueness* half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` is technically reachable from the iter-002 `eq_of_eqOnOpen` once `Jacobian C` lands.
7	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean` — **closed iter-002**. 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
8	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean` — **closed iter-002** (under documented first-approximation). 0 sorries. Status: DONE. Bespoke invertible-quasi-coherent definition gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (multi-iteration; not in scope until upstream Mathlib).
9	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` — **closed iter-003**. 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
10	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` — definition closed iter-003; 1 deferred sorry remaining: `PicardFunctor.representable` (L185). Status: DEFERRED — closing on the `LineBundle` first-approximation would silently assert representability of the wrong functor (forbidden shortcut). Honest closure requires `LineBundle` refinement (gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`) plus the FGA argument. Multi-iteration; do not retry until both are in motion.
11	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean` — **closed iter-004**. 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
12	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` — closed across iter-004/005/007/008. 0 sorries. Status: DONE.
13	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` — closed across iter-005/006/007/009/010/012/014/015 (carrier definitions + Čech scaffolding); iter-027 split removed the iter-016 → iter-026 LES cohort to `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`. 0 sorries; 299 LOC. Status: DONE.
14	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` — created iter-027 (refactor subagent, mechanical split). 0 sorries; 957 LOC. Hosts the 23 iter-016 → iter-026 LES infrastructure declarations verbatim, the iter-028 `Scheme.AffineCoverMVSquare` structure + `toMayerVietorisSquare` accessor, the iter-029 cohort (four `@[simp]` corner-identification lemmas + sheaf-parameterised `HModule'_sequence` LES specialisation + exactness lemma), the iter-030 cohort (toModuleKSheaf-specialisation of the LES on a curve `HModule'_sequence_curve` + exactness companion `HModule'_sequence_curve_exact`), the iter-031 cohort (cover-totality source-object iso `HModule'_top_sourceIso` in `section CoverTotality`), the iter-032 cohort (cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u+1` `HModule_top_linearEquiv`, also in `section CoverTotality`), the iter-033 cohort (parallel cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u` `HModule'_top_linearEquiv`, also in `section CoverTotality`), the iter-034 cohort (universe-bump Mathlib gap-fill `Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` at the top of the file before `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` + full cover-totality bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` inside `section CoverTotality`), and the iter-035 cohort (corner-bridge specialisation `AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv` + curve specialisation `_curve` inside `section CoverTotality`; closed iter-035, see `task_done.md`). Status: DONE for iter-016 → iter-026/028/029/030/031/032/033/034/035 cohorts; **iter-036 (this iteration) appends two new declarations** specialising the iter-035 corner-bridge LinearEquiv into a finrank equality via Mathlib's `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`: (i) `AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄` (abstract sheaf form) and (ii) `AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄_curve` (curve specialisation via `toModuleKSheaf C`); see Iter-036 plan below.
15	
16	## Iter-027 refactor (executed; verified iter-028)
17	
18	Mechanical file split: 23 iter-016→iter-026 LES declarations migrated verbatim from `StructureSheafModuleK.lean` to `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`; `lean_diagnostic_messages` returned `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`; axioms kernel-only; sorry count `9 → 9` (no proof bodies altered); blueprint updated (`Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` extracted with `\leanok` markers preserved, `content.tex` updated). Two minor blueprint reconstruction divergences: iter-020 lemma consolidation; two label renames in iter-023/iter-026 (recorded in session-36 summary.md). Migrated to `task_done.md`.
19	
20	## Iter-036 plan (this iteration — finrank corollary of the corner-bridge)
21	
22	**Iter-035 closure verification (this pass).** Plan-agent independently verified the iter-035 prover's two-declaration close (full report in `task_done.md`):
23	- `lean_diagnostic_messages AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` (file is 957 LOC, up from 915 — `+42` LOC; in line with the +25 plan estimate plus docstrings + new `import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preorder`).
24	- `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv` returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only.
25	- `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv_curve` returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only.
26	- Sorry count remains 9 (no transient scaffold sorries; iter-035 prover landed both declarations in one Edit, twenty-seventh consecutive prover round of refactor + prover collapse).
27	- `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged; no new `axiom` declarations; `Genus.lean` still kernel-only.
28	- The bodies use the verbatim probe-confirmed patterns (corner-bridge: term-mode one-liner via `Eq.symm.▸ Preorder.isTerminalTop`; curve: dot-notation `S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv k _ n`).
29	
30	**Iter-036 candidate scoping (this pass).** With Step 3.5 closed (the iter-035 corner-bridge `LinearEquiv` `HModule' k F n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄ ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n` in scope), the natural follow-on is the immediate **finrank corollary** — the algebraic stepping stone that lands the genus carrier $\mathrm{finrank}_k\,(\HModule\,k\,(\toModuleKSheaf\,C)\,1)$ on the explicit corner $X_4$ of any 2-affine cover, ready for the affine-vanishing argument of iter-037+. The plan-agent probes the body in detail (this pass):
31	
32	1. **`LinearEquiv.finrank_eq` resolution probe**: Mathlib provides `theorem LinearEquiv.finrank_eq {R} {M N} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N] (f : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : Module.finrank R M = Module.finrank R N` (`Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Finrank.lean`). The `HModule k F n` and `HModule' k F n X₄` carriers are `Type u` with `AddCommGroup` + `Module k` inherited via the `Linear k` enrichment + `Abelian.Ext.instModule` (probe-confirmed iter-009 / iter-014). `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq` applies directly to iter-035's corner-bridge.
33	
34	2. **Probe-confirmed body, abstract sheaf form** (this pass; verbatim text in *Current Objectives* of `PROGRESS.md`):
35	   - **Abstract sheaf form**: `theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄` returns `Module.finrank k (HModule k F n) = Module.finrank k (HModule' k F n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄)` via `(S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv k F n).symm.finrank_eq`.
36	   - **Curve specialisation**: `theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄_curve` returns `Module.finrank k (HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n) = Module.finrank k (HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄)` via `S.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄ k _ n` (dot-notation method-call form).
37	   - Both bodies probe-confirmed: `lean_run_code` returns `{success: true, diagnostics: []}` and `#print axioms` returns `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — kernel-only.
38	
39	3. **`.symm.finrank_eq` orientation requirement** (this pass): iter-035's bridge is `HModule' k F n X₄ ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n` (LHS = X₄ form, RHS = global form). `.finrank_eq` directly would give `finrank k (HModule' k F n X₄) = finrank k (HModule k F n)`. The genus formula `genus C := Module.finrank k (HModule k _ 1)` puts the global form on the LHS, so we want `finrank k (HModule k F n) = finrank k (HModule' k F n X₄)` — this requires `.symm.finrank_eq`. Probe-confirmed.
40	
41	4. **`HasExt.{u}` and `HasExt.{u + 1}` both required as explicit hypotheses**: same plan-agent reasoning as iter-034 / iter-035. Whitespace `[HasExt.{u + 1} ...]` (linter-required spacing).
42	
43	5. **No new imports required** (this pass): `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq` lives in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Finrank.lean`, transitively in scope via the existing imports of `MayerVietoris.lean`. Probe-confirmed: `lean_run_code` test against the post-iter-035 file with no extra imports compiles cleanly.
44	
45	**Iter-036 work scope.** Append two declarations to `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` inside the existing `section CoverTotality` (after iter-035's `HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv_curve`, before `end CoverTotality`):
46	
47	1. `theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄` — abstract sheaf-parameterised finrank equality.
48	2. `theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄_curve` — `toModuleKSheaf C` curve specialisation.
49	
50	Total expected source change: ~25 LOC append (two theorem declarations + load-bearing docstrings). No protected files affected. Sorry count: `9 → 9` (no transient scaffold sorries — both bodies are probe-confirmed term-mode one-liners).
51	
52	Blueprint: chapter `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Finrank corollary of the corner-bridge (iter-036)* (added this pass) — theorems `thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_finrank_HModule_eq_HModule_prime_X4` and `thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_finrank_HModule_eq_HModule_prime_X4_curve`.
53	
54	**No Step closes/opens with iter-036.** Iter-036 is a thin algebraic stepping stone bridging the iter-035 LinearEquiv and the iter-037+ affine-vanishing extraction (Step 4) and `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) i)` (Step 5).
55	
56	**Refactor + prover sub-phase collapse acceptable.** The iter-036 prover may collapse refactor + prover into a single Edit (twenty-eighth consecutive prover round at this rate).
57	
58	## Earlier closed plan entries
59	
60	- **iter-035** (closed): Two append cohorts inside `section CoverTotality` after iter-034's `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` — (i) `AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv` (abstract sheaf form, body `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv k F n (S.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄.symm ▸ Preorder.isTerminalTop (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat))`); (ii) `AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv_curve` (curve specialisation via `S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv k _ n` dot-notation method-call form). Single new import added at top of file: `import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preorder`. Both kernel-only, +42 LOC (915 → 957), sorry count `9 → 9`. Step 3.5 closed.
61	- **iter-034** (closed): Two append cohorts — (i) `Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (Mathlib gap-fill at top of file before `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, two `private` helper lemmas `chgUniv_add` / `chgUniv_smul` + 7-line `LinearEquiv` structure literal; universe binders extended `universe u v` → `universe u v w w'`); (ii) `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` (inside `section CoverTotality`, after iter-033's `HModule'_top_linearEquiv`, one-liner `.trans`); both kernel-only, +99 LOC (816 → 915), sorry count `9 → 9`. Step 3 closed.
62	- **iter-033** (closed): Appended `HModule'_top_linearEquiv` inside `section CoverTotality` — cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u` (universe-`u` parallel of iter-032 `HModule_top_linearEquiv`) via `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two `precompOfLinear` directions through iter-031's `HModule'_top_sourceIso`; four-step rewrite chain per round-trip; no explicit `Abelian.Ext.{u}` annotation needed (LHS forces `Ext.{u}`); closed kernel-only, +48 LOC (816 LOC total), sorry count 9 unchanged.
63	- **iter-032** (closed): Appended `HModule_top_linearEquiv` — Ext-transport at universe `u+1` via `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two `precompOfLinear` directions through iter-031's `HModule'_top_sourceIso`; four-step rewrite chain per round-trip (`← comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero` → `mk₀_comp_mk₀` → iso identity → `mk₀_id_comp`); `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}` annotation load-bearing (matches `HModule : Type (u+1)`); closed kernel-only, +48 LOC (768 LOC total), sorry count 9 unchanged.
64	- **iter-031** (closed): Appended `HModule'_top_sourceIso` in new `section CoverTotality` — cover-totality source-object iso composing three Mathlib pieces under `(presheafToSheaf J _).mapIso`: terminal-collapse via `IsTerminal.from`/`hom_ext` + `Functor.constComp _ PUnit (ModuleCat.free k)` + `(Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).mapIso (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso`; mirrors `constantSheaf = const ⋙ presheafToSheaf`; closed kernel-only, +43 LOC (720 LOC total), sorry count 9 unchanged.
65	
66	## Iter-037+ candidate scoping (informational only, not assigned this iteration)
67	
68	After iter-036's `AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄` + `_curve` land, the iter-037+ plan-agent will scope the next semantic step. Plausible next targets in priority order:
69	
70	1. **`Module.Finite` transport companion**: as a thin algebraic counterpart to iter-036's finrank corollary — `theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` plus its `_curve` form, body via `Module.Finite.equiv (S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv k F n)`. Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe (iter-036 pass) confirms both bodies typecheck kernel-only — **so iter-037 can close immediately**, modulo a final plan-agent re-scoping.
71	2. **Serre-vanishing on affines**: the input lemma `H^{>0}(Spec A, F) = 0` for the `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology. Larger scope (could be multi-iteration if Mathlib lacks the affine vanishing theorem at the right level of generality — Mathlib has only abstract `RightDerived` machinery, no scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines); plan-agent re-probe required first.
72	3. **`Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) i)`** (the eventual target — Step 5). Multi-iteration. Consumes 1 plus iter-030's LES + iter-035's corner bridge.
73	
74	Independently, Phase B (sheaf of relative differentials) and the iter-002-closed Phase E rigidity helper remain available for parallel opening if the Path-2 chain stalls.
75	
76	## User-facing decision RESOLVED (logged iter 008, resolved iter 011)
77	
78	The user (`2026-05-07`, recorded in early `USER_HINTS.md` entries) authorised agents to add the `noncomputable` modifier to the three protected `def`-flavoured declarations — `genus`, `Jacobian`, `Jacobian.ofCurve` — when required for honest closure. The omission in `references/challenge.lean` is treated as an oversight; argument types, names, and order are preserved verbatim. Iter-011 used the authorisation to close `genus` (added `noncomputable` modifier; `noncomputable def genus … := Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`).
79	
80	## Mathlib gap (recorded once, applies to all three protected files)
81	
82	**Phase C representability.** `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` is **still absent** in current Mathlib. Iter-028 plan-agent did not re-probe (no Mathlib advance on this front is expected this round); the gap continues to gate `LineBundle` refinement and hence `PicardFunctor.representable`. Multi-iteration; deferred indefinitely.
83	
84	**Phase A step 6 *proper* (Serre finiteness on a proper curve).** Mathlib has the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`) on `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat`-valued cohomology, the abstract `MayerVietorisSquare` API, and the Čech-cochain-complex carrier (`cechComplexFunctor`). It does **not** have:
85	- A Čech-to-derived-functor comparison theorem (no `cechCohomology_iso_derivedFunctor`-style lemma; iter-028 plan-agent re-probe confirms still gated).
86	- An affine-vanishing `H^{>0}(Spec A, F) = 0` for quasi-coherent sheaves on schemes (Mathlib has only the `EllAdicCohomology` and abstract `RightDerived` machinery; no scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines).
87	- Any concept of `Coherent` or `QuasiCoherent` for sheaves of `O_X`-modules at the scheme level (`KaehlerDifferential` is rings-only).
88	
89	The iter-014 → iter-026 cohort built the `ModuleCat k`-flavored MV-LES infrastructure end-to-end. Iter-028 (this iteration) opens the geometric-side scaffolding (the bundled 2-affine cover); iter-029+ continues with cover-totality identifications and affine-vanishing extractions. The eventual Serre-finiteness theorem `Module.Finite k (HModule k F i)` for `F = toModuleKSheaf C` on a proper geometrically integral $k$-curve is what `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (one of the four `Jacobian.lean` instances) eventually consumes.
90	
91	**Phase E rigidity.** The Mumford §4 rigidity theorem for morphisms between abelian varieties — gating the *uniqueness* half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` — has the helper `eq_of_eqOnOpen` (closed iter-002) but not the full Albanese-style universal property. Multi-iteration; deferred until `Jacobian C` lands.
92	
93	## Forbidden shortcuts (documented once)
94	
95	- `genus C := 0` or any constant. Resolved iter-011.
96	- `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec _))`. Forbidden.
97	- `ofCurve P := toUnit C ≫ η[Jacobian C]`. Forbidden.
98	- `LineBundle X := Unit / PUnit / X.Modules`. Forbidden (would force `Pic X = 0`).
99	- `PicardFunctor C := Discrete PUnit`. Forbidden (vacuous functor).
100	- Closing `PicardFunctor.representable` on the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle`. Forbidden (silently asserts the wrong functor).
101	- Closing the Phase A step 2–4 / step 5 prerequisites / step 5 *main* / step 5 polish / step 6 *Path 2* / iter-027 / iter-028 sorries with vacuous shortcuts (`decide` / `trivial` / vacuous-presheaf / vacuous-sheaf / vacuous-rank / `Sorry`). Forbidden.
102	- Closing Phase C step 3 codomain change with a constant-zero functor. Forbidden.
103	- Closing the `forgetCompare` natural iso with a non-natural iso between unrelated functors. Forbidden.
104	- Closing the eventual Phase A step 6 (Serre finiteness) with `decide`/`trivial`/vacuous-rank values. Forbidden.
105	- Closing iter-009 / iter-014 carriers with `def` instead of `noncomputable abbrev`. Forbidden (instance synthesis fails).
106	- Closing iter-010 / iter-015 H⁰ bridges with `≃` or `≃+` instead of `≃ₗ[k]`. Forbidden.
107	- Closing iter-016/017/018/019/020/021/022/023/026 deliverables with anything other than the verbatim Mathlib mirrors. Forbidden.
108	- Renaming any of the 23 iter-016 → iter-026 declarations. Forbidden (downstream code paths assume `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_*` spellings).
109	- Renaming the iter-028 fields (`U₁`, `U₂`, `isAffineOpen_U₁`, `isAffineOpen_U₂`, `isAffineOpen_inf`, `cover`) or the accessor (`toMayerVietorisSquare`). Forbidden (downstream Serre-finiteness lemmas assume these spellings).
110	- Dropping the `noncomputable` modifier from iter-028 `toMayerVietorisSquare` — Mathlib's `Opens.mayerVietorisSquare` is `noncomputable`; the wrapper inherits the requirement (resolved iter-028).
111	- Adding `@[simps]` or wrapping in `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` on iter-028 declarations — probe-confirmed unnecessary (resolved iter-028).
